titleStmt.title: The Ambassador in China ( (Gauss) ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The Ambassador in China ( Gauss ) to the Secretary of State
div.p: Sir: I have the honor to transmit a translation,Not printed. supplied by the International Department of the Central Publicity Board, of Generalissimo Chiang Kai-shek’s message to the Chinese nation on September 18, 1941, the tenth anniversary of the Mukden Incident.

The speech affirms the unity of the four northeastern provinces with the rest of China, says that their riches must not be permitted to strengthen the forces of the aggressors, and announces China’s determination to fight until they are again an unqualified part of China. Reference is made to the objectives of the anti-aggression powers, as
outlined in the Roosevelt-Churchill Eight Points,Declaration of Principles, known as the Atlantic Charter, by the President of the United States of America and the Prime Minister of the United Kingdom, August 14, 1941, Department of State Bulletin, August 16, 1941, p. 125. and to America’s leadership in recognizing and aiding China’s part in the common struggle.
